confirmed.title: ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN not scrubbed from SDK subprocess environment
confirmed.verification: Verified in /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/src/reasoning_client.py:309 - _scrubbed_env() filters only ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, not ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN. Confirmed via Anthropic SDK source (v0.109.1, installed at .venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/anthropic/_client.py:213-214) that both env vars are read and used for auth. Confirmed in claude-agent-sdk subprocess flow (.venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/claude_agent_sdk/_internal/transport/subprocess_cli.py:430-434) that env dict is merged and passed unfiltered to subprocess. Test coverage at tests/test_reasoning_client.py:70-75 only validates ANTHROPIC_API_KEY removal. Issue not documented as WONTFIX in HARDENING_LOG.md Pass 7 (lines 291-304).
confirmed.recommended_action: Modify _scrubbed_env() at line 309 to exclude both ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN from the subprocess environment. Add unit test to verify both credentials are removed (extend test_scrubbed_env_removes_metered_key to cover the alternate token). This ensures the SDK subprocess cannot use an alternate metered credential to violate ADR-0016's fixed-budget constraint.
confirmed.proposed_fix:
Modify _scrubbed_env() to exclude both ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN:

```python
def _scrubbed_env() -> dict[str, str]:
    """Environment for the Agent SDK subprocess with Anthropic credentials removed.
    
    ADR-0016: execution runs entirely on the fixed Claude Max subscription via the
    `claude` CLI's OAuth token. A metered Anthropic API key/token must NEVER be used.
    """
    return {k: v for k, v in os.environ.items() 
            if k not in ("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY", "ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN")}
```

Add a unit test to verify both credentials are removed.

confirmed.title: Missing llm.temperature span attribute in reasoning.complete span
confirmed.verification:
Verified via /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/src/reasoning_client.py:
- Lines 488: complete() accepts temperature: float = 0.0
- Line 553: temperature is passed to _attempt() inside the retry loop
- Lines 524-544: span initialization sets llm.provider, llm.model_name, llm.system, input.value, etc. BUT NEVER sets llm.temperature
- Lines 641-658: complete_json() calls complete() with varying temperature (attempt_temp, lines 647-652 compute distinct temps per retry) — this creates child spans without temperature attribute
- No grep matches for "llm.temperature" or "set_attribute.*temperature" anywhere in reasoning_client.py
- Pass-5 batch 16 added temperature-retry bumping logic (#275) but never added the span attribute to track it
- Pass-10 batch 21 added json.parse_attempts to failure path for observability parity, but still no temperature

The issue is REAL, REACHABLE (occurs when complete_json retries on JSON parse failure), and CONFIRMED. However, severity should be LOW not MEDIUM because: (1) it is not a runtime bug — the code works correctly, (2) it is purely observability polish, (3) temperature defaults to 0.0 in most call paths and only varies within complete_json retry loop, (4) per pass-8 calibration notes, observability/test-coverage items should be capped at low, (5) missing a single optional tracing attribute is not comparable to correctness/data-sovereignty/critical-path bugs.

This is honest-severity observability polish, similar in nature to pass-10's json.parse_attempts symmetry fix (which was marked medium because it was about failure-path parity on an already-implemented feature; this is about a missing optional parameter).
confirmed.recommended_action: Consider adding `if temperature != 0.0: span.set_attribute("llm.temperature", temperature)` in complete() after line 544, or unconditionally set it for consistency (temperature may be non-zero on T2 Ollama calls). However, given the low severity and pass-8/pass-10 convergence notes indicating the audit has reached the noise floor, this could be deferred or marked WONTFIX depending on team priorities. If fixed, add a test verifying temperature is captured on complete() spans when complete_json() retries.
confirmed.proposed_fix: In complete() function around line 544 (after setting input.value), add: `if temperature != 0.0: span.set_attribute("llm.temperature", temperature)` (or always set it unconditionally for consistency, since it may be non-zero on T2 Ollama calls). The temperature value passed to _attempt() should be captured and set on the span before the retry loop to ensure it reflects the actual decoding parameter.

confirmed.title: requirements.txt has inconsistent version constraints for pytest/pytest-cov vs requirements-ci.txt and pyproject.toml
confirmed.verification:
Verified in actual code:
- /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/requirements.txt:98-99 uses exact pins: pytest==8.2.2, pytest-cov==5.0.0
- /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/requirements-ci.txt:31-32 uses bounded ranges: pytest>=8.2.2,<9 and pytest-cov>=5.0.0,<6
- /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/pyproject.toml:41-42 uses bounded ranges: pytest>=8.2.2,<9 and pytest-cov>=5.0.0,<6

Requirements.txt header (lines 1-4) claims "floors, not exact ==" but the file contradicts itself with exact == pins.

Pass-8 (commit 85559f8, 2026-06-15) updated pyproject.toml [dev] to add the <9 and <6 ceilings to match requirements-ci.txt, but left requirements.txt unchanged.

Requirements-ci.txt lines 28-30 explicitly document the intent: "Major-version ceilings so CI runs the same major as production (requirements.txt pins pytest==8.2.2 / pytest-cov==5.0.0); without these, pip resolves pytest to 9.x and CI no longer exercises the version the node runs (#264 pattern, pass-5)."

This is unmitigated — there is no validation or override mechanism that enforces version consistency across the three dependency specifications.
confirmed.recommended_action: Update /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/requirements.txt lines 98-99 from exact pins to bounded ranges to match requirements-ci.txt and pyproject.toml: change pytest==8.2.2 to pytest>=8.2.2,<9 and pytest-cov==5.0.0 to pytest-cov>=5.0.0,<6. This restores the parity principle stated in requirements-ci.txt (lines 28-30) and aligns with the requirements.txt header comment claiming "floors, not exact ==". Without this fix, developers using -r requirements.txt diverge from CI/dev installs.
confirmed.dimension: xc-config (cross-layer: config.py schema validation, platform.yaml consumption, requirements*.txt bounds/consistency, pyproject.toml build)
confirmed.proposed_fix: Update requirements.txt lines 98-99 to use bounded ranges like requirements-ci.txt and pyproject.toml: change `pytest==8.2.2` to `pytest>=8.2.2,<9` and `pytest-cov==5.0.0` to `pytest-cov>=5.0.0,<6`. This restores parity and prevents accidental major-version drift. The comment at the top of requirements.txt (lines 1-4) says 'floors, not exact ==', so using floors here is consistent with that guidance.

confirmed.title: Unclamped timeout environment variables cause ValueError on negative overrides
confirmed.verification: Read src/reasoning_client.py lines 95-96: T2_TIMEOUT and T3_TIMEOUT are defined as float(_env(...)) with no validation. Lines 107, 111, 114 show BACKOFF_BASE, RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF, and MAX_RETRIES ARE clamped with max(). Tested with HCGRC_T2_TIMEOUT=-120 in subprocess: confirmed that negative timeout is accepted at module load, then raises ValueError("Timeout value out of range") at line 238/265 when ChatOllama.invoke() calls the HTTP client (socket.settimeout(negative)). This ValueError is caught by the generic except block at line 287 and wrapped as ReasoningError at line 294. For T3_TIMEOUT, a negative value is passed to asyncio.wait_for at line 396, which raises asyncio.TimeoutError instead. The error IS caught, but only at call time, not at module-load time (unlike BACKOFF_BASE which fails fast). Error message is confusing: "timed out after -110s". No test exists for timeout validation like test_backoff_bases_clamped_non_negative (line 460). This is not in the documented WONTFIX list from Pass 7.
confirmed.recommended_action: Clamp both T2_TIMEOUT and T3_TIMEOUT to positive minimums at module-load time, mirroring the pattern used for BACKOFF_BASE and RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF. Example: T2_TIMEOUT = max(0.1, float(_env("HCGRC_T2_TIMEOUT", "120"))) to ensure a minimum of 0.1s. Add a test case test_timeout_values_clamped_non_negative following the pattern of test_backoff_bases_clamped_non_negative (line 460) to verify negative overrides are rejected at module import.
confirmed.proposed_fix: Clamp both T2_TIMEOUT and T3_TIMEOUT to positive minimums at module-load time, mirroring the pattern used for BACKOFF_BASE and RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF. Example: `T2_TIMEOUT = max(0.1, float(_env(...)))` to ensure a minimum of 0.1s and reject negative values immediately. This catches misconfiguration at import time rather than at call time, and prevents ValueError from being wrapped and obscured in exception handling.
